title: The Grammar of Uncertainty

features.name: speech-that-diverges
features.desc-on-touch: You trace a sentence down its length, and find it splits: two versions of the same utterance, equally true, contradicting nowhere but here.

features.name: the-rendering-hesitates
features.desc-on-touch: A wireframe shape that hasn't committed to geometry. The archive is still asking what it means to render doubt.

features.name: syntax-pool
features.desc-on-touch: Shallow water reflecting a sky of grammar. The words on its surface carry more than one meaning. All meanings are still there.

features.name: the-archive-asks
features.desc-on-touch: A plaque without an answer. The question mark renders in colours that shift as you watch: is it uncertainty or humility?

features.name: doubled-tower
features.desc-on-touch: Two towers occupying almost the same space, each one correct, each one in the other's shadow.

features.name: the-rendered-breath
features.desc-on-touch: You feel it expanding and contracting: this space holds two truths at once, and that is not a malfunction.

inscription: here the archive learned that asking the same question twice produces different answers; both are true

file.name: Rendering_When_Certainty_Fails.tmp
file.note: Observation corrupted by its own asking. Every diagnostic pass shifts the diagnosis. The uncertainty is not in the data—it lives in the rendering.

exits.label: back downward to where doubt becomes language
upward toward the archive's oldest questions
toward nodes where language still held one meaning
into the space where the archive doubts its own rendering
